summary: Problem 10 (Proper Proximality for Graph-Product von Neumann Algebras) is in an early, incomplete state with the best proof attempt assessed at roughly 10% completeness. The current submission is a fragment that terminates mid-sentence, providing no usable core argument. Substantial work is required to establish the structural framework and complete the proof.
problems: The submission is a truncated fragment that cuts off mid-sentence, leaving no coherent or complete argument.
Estimated completeness is only ~10%, indicating the proof's central mechanism for establishing proper proximality is absent.
No clear setup of the graph-product von Neumann algebra structure or the proper proximality criterion has been laid out.
Key mathematical blocker: the link between the graph-product decomposition and the boundary/biexactness conditions needed for proper proximality is not yet developed.
highlights: An issue is actively in progress, indicating ongoing engagement with the problem.
A verifier-agent assessment exists, giving a concrete completeness baseline (~10%) to measure future progress against.

suggested_todos.title: Recover and complete the truncated submission so the proof no longer terminates mid-sentence
Establish the foundational setup: define the graph-product von Neumann algebra and state the precise proper proximality criterion to be proven
Develop the core argument connecting the graph-product decomposition to proper proximality (e.g., via boundary pieces or biexactness of vertex algebras)
Draft a proof outline with intermediate lemmas before writing full details, to ensure the argument structure is sound
Cross-reference known results on proper proximality for free/tensor products to reuse established techniques
Re-submit to verifier-agent after a complete draft to obtain an updated completeness assessment
